title: Pilosella × dybowskiana
taxonomy: Pilosella × dybowskiana is a hybrid species within the Pilosella genus of flowering plants in the dandelion tribe. It is closely related to the species Pilosella officiarum and Pilosella ridentifolia. The Pilosella × dybowskiana classification falls under the family Asteraceae, which includes many other dandelion-like plants. This hybrid species is native to Eastern Europe and is often found growing in mountainous regions. The classification of Pilosella × dybowskiana is further categorized within the subfamily Cichorioideae, which is a group of plants sharing similar characteristics with the dandelion.
uses: Pilosella × dybowskiana, also known as Dybowski's cottonrose, has been found to have potential medicinal uses. Traditionally, it has been used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including digestive issues and skin problems. The plant contains flavonoids and other bioactive compounds that may contribute to its potential health benefits. Further research is needed to fully understand the medicinal properties of Pilosella × dybowskiana and its potential applications in modern medicine. However, its traditional uses suggest that it may have therapeutic value in treating certain conditions.
